description: On a tree, ladder, or steep slope, 'kalei' is upward movement sustained by the climber's effort and contact with the route. 'rihe' (rise) reports upward change without choosing this manner.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k', release it into central 'a', then place the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for voiced lateral 'l'. Stress the middle-height front 'e'. Keep final high-front 'i' in a separate syllable: raise the tongue after 'e' without turning the two vowels into a glide.
sound_symbolism: The vowels move from open 'a' through middle-height 'e' to high 'i'. Giving the last vowel its own small foothold makes the word itself seem to climb.
usage_notes: The climbed feature can stand as the direct object. A destination may instead take 'kau' (ALL), and a direction not yet completed takes 'wea' (TOWARD).

semantic_domains.activity: effortful ascent along a supporting route
semantic_domains.physical: bodily movement maintained through successive contact
semantic_domains.spatial: travel from a lower position towards a higher one
